first finish what you were working on. then make sure you are finishing the system and building any integration needed to be competitive in a universal generative automation control plane business designed to utilize all of the features that exist in the modules in the murphy system/murphy_integrated folder. Also research the top features the system will need to be competitive and apply them to the system. Male sure you first are analyzing what needs to be done, then making a plan to perform those tasks then test those tasks, then review the tests, then fix it again if needed which includes a follow up test as a loop until it is working as expected in tests, then move to the next task. Make sure standardization exists through the system and also make sure we are applying .gitignore where applicable so the git file system isn't getting larger and largetr with no longer active notes. organize what is there like a professional team of software designers using the proper terminology working together. and prioritize features that will enable the system to also work on itself and improve itself. Make sure at the end of every task that you update the readme for the project. This needs to reflect the state of the system every time you are finishing with a request.
